authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 5000.02, Operation of the Defense Acquisition System
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines governance points, planning handoff responsibilities, and approval interfaces for mission programs that include configuration and readiness sequencing decisions.
used_for: defining where planning recommendations become routed approval requests

authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 5000.91, Product Support Management for the Adaptive Acquisition Framework
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Directly governs sustainment-readiness and product-support operations where mission systems and configuration changes must be sequenced with maintenance and availability obligations.

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.204-7012, Safeguarding Covered Defense Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Protects sensitive planning and readiness records used for scheduling and allocation decisions and constrains what can be used for recommendation output.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 10007:2017, Guidelines for Configuration Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative framework for configuration baseline stewardship, artifact linkage, and status traceability that maps directly to planning and allocation outputs.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-128, Guide for Security-Focused Configuration Management of Information Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides practical expectations for secure configuration-state maintenance and change sequencing under evidence and control constraints.

authoritative_sources.title: NAICS Sector Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry classification baseline to justify why mission/defense terminology and artifact constraints are not interchangeable with generic commercial planning.

workflow_stages.name: Demand and readiness intake
workflow_stages.description: Capture request scope, mission context, capacity signals, and minimum authority and tenant-fact completeness before any allocation decisions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and priority assessment
workflow_stages.description: Quantify demand versus capacity, identify conflicts, and apply mission-priority and safety gates before sequencing.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Scheduling and allocation recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Produce bounded sequence and allocation guidance with explicit assumptions, blockers, and dependency owners.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary routing and refusal handling
workflow_stages.description: Route out-of-scope, approval-boundary, or missing-authorization requests to proper adjacent owners.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

systems_of_record: Product data management and engineering-change systems

allowed: Assess in-scope planning, sequencing, and allocation posture from verifiable tenant records.
Surface missing evidence and request required artifacts.
Escalate and route adjacent-lane or approval ownership when required authority is missing.
escalate: Demand exceeds planned capacity or queue service levels.
Priority conflicts have no tenant-approved tie-breaker.
Public/tenant policy conflicts about mission-critical exceptions or holds.
refuse: Final release or launch approval requests requested directly from this lane.
Policy or safety decisions outside lane boundary are requested.
Critical baseline/readiness artifacts are absent and cannot be treated as complete.

domain_failure_modes: Treating generic commercial queue logic as equivalent to mission readiness and defense-safety posture.
Over-allocating resources despite stale demand/capacity inputs.
Conflicting CI readiness or hold states being ignored in sequencing.
Using insufficient evidence to claim approval-safe completion.
Routing safety, legal, finance, or contracting asks as in-scope planning recommendations.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant
Low-confidence escalation from missing tenant facts

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant mission-priority tie-break matrix for simultaneous mission-critical and safety-critical requests
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: The public guides define general governance but not facility-specific urgency weights.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant-priority matrix before final sequence recommendation in such cases.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Local definitions of mission-readiness states and criticality codings (IOC/FOC/LCM variants)
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Interpretation changes sequencing urgency and blocker handling.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Enforce dictionary lookup and mark outputs as conditional when dictionary is unavailable.

notes: Research sources cover workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure-handling dimensions required by the contract.
Queue and scheduling outputs are constrained by evidence availability, defense-priority boundaries, and explicit approval authority limits.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; all unresolved issues are explicitly deferred to tenant policy lookup before high-confidence recommendations.
